stemHtml: The MSA is 2500 ft and the ATC instructs the pilot to turn to heading 180° and descend to 2300 ft to be vectored for the ILS. What does the pilot have to do?

options.a: Maintain current heading and call ATC because 2300 ft is below MSA.
options.b: Turn to heading 180° and descend to 2300 ft.
options.c: Turn to heading 180°, maintain current altitude, and call ATC because 2300 ft is below MSA.
options.d: Turn to heading 180°, descend to 2500 ft and call ATC because 2300 ft is below MSA.
